# value-vision usage guide for AI agents

`value-vision` (package `@cdlab/values`) is a client-only, statically-exported
Next.js App Router app: a purchasing-power converter where crypto, fiat, and
fixed-price products share one board. Typing an amount into any field re-prices
every other field. Live rates are fetched in the browser (CoinGecko crypto +
exchangerate-api fiat), cross-rated through USD into an N×N matrix, with a
bundled snapshot as an offline fallback. No backend, no database, no accounts,
no secrets.

Primary docs:

- README: ./README.md
- Design spec (authoritative): ./DESIGN.md

Part of the `@cdlab/projects-monorepo`; run all commands from the repo root with
`pnpm --filter @cdlab/values <script>`.

## What it is

- **`output: 'export'` static SPA** — no server runtime (no `app/api`, no route
  handlers, no middleware). Entry: `src/app/layout.tsx` (bg effects, metadata,
  GA) + `src/app/page.tsx` (`'use client'`, the single converter page).
- **Two hooks drive everything.** `useExchangeRates` (rate matrix: fetch +
  fallback + refresh) and `useCurrencyConverter(rates)` (board state +
  conversion). `page.tsx` wires them.
- **Deploy target: Cloudflare Pages** via `@cloudflare/next-on-pages`
  (`build:cf`). Live at https://values.pages.dev/. No `wrangler.jsonc`, no
  bindings.

## Data flow

On mount / manual refresh (`useExchangeRates.ts`):

1. `ExchangeRate.fetchExchangeRates()` runs `Promise.all([fetchCryptoPrices(),
   fetchFiatRates()])` — CoinGecko + exchangerate-api in parallel.
2. `getProductPrices(fiatRates)` — each product's fixed price ÷ its home fiat
   rate = USD price (or the price directly when home currency is USD).
3. `calculateCrossRates()` builds the dense N×N matrix; `1` on the diagonal,
   every pair via `calculateRate` (all routed through USD). Unhandled pair ⇒ `0`.
4. On any fetch error: catch, set error, `setRates(defaultRates)` (bundled
   fallback, `src/lib/rates.ts`).

User types (`useCurrencyConverter.ts`):

5. `handleAmountChange(index, value)` updates the field, records
   `lastInputIndex`, schedules `convert(index, newFields)` via `setTimeout(0)`
   (passes fresh fields to avoid stale closure).
6. `convert` reads the source, `parseAmount` (strips commas), and for every OTHER
   field: same currency ⇒ copy; else `amount × rates[from][to]`; else `0`.
   Formats to 2 decimals via `toLocaleString`.
7. A `useEffect` keyed on `rates` re-converts `lastInputIndex` when new rates
   arrive.

## Key files

- `src/app/page.tsx` — the board; wires the two hooks, renders one row
  (`CurrencySelector` + `AmountInput`) per field.
- `src/app/layout.tsx` — metadata, JSON-LD, GA `G-FPHG7CDDVQ`, full-screen
  `Plasma` + 400-particle `Particles` background, dark default theme.
- `src/app/error.tsx` — error boundary (copy still says "storefront" — leftover
  template text).
- `src/hooks/useExchangeRates.ts` — fetch + parallel APIs + `defaultRates`
  fallback + `refreshRates`.
- `src/hooks/useCurrencyConverter.ts` — field state, `convert`, distinct-currency
  helper `getUsedCurrencies`.
- `src/lib/currencies.ts` — `CURRENCY_CONFIG` (crypto/fiat/products) +
  `DEFAULT_FIELDS`.
- `src/lib/exchangeRate.ts` — `ExchangeRate` class; live fetch + all cross-rate
  math. ONLY file that hits the network.
- `src/lib/rates.ts` — `defaultRates` bundled fallback matrix.
- `src/types/currency.ts` — `CurrencyConfig`, `ExchangeRates` (`{[from]:{[to]:
  number}}`), `ConverterField`, API response shapes.
- `src/components/{AmountInput,CurrencySelector}.tsx`,
  `src/components/layout/*` — presentational; `client-providers.tsx` holds theme
  + version badge.

## Config & bindings

- **No runtime config, no secrets, no bindings.** Only build input is
  `BUILD_TIME` (`next.config.ts` `env` → `client-providers.tsx` footer badge).
- Rate APIs are keyless, hardcoded in `exchangeRate.ts`: CoinGecko
  `api/v3/simple/price` (crypto USD) + exchangerate-api `v4/latest/USD` (fiat).
- `next.config.ts`: `output:'export'`, `images.unoptimized`, remote host
  whitelist (`res.cloudinary.com`, `wcd.pages.dev`).

## Gotchas for agents

- **Static export = client-only.** `page.tsx` is `'use client'`; rate fetching is
  browser-side and CORS-dependent. Do NOT add server code, `app/api`, or a proxy
  — `output:'export'` forbids a server runtime.
- **No caching/retry/debounce** on fetches; refresh re-fetches everything.
  CoinGecko's public endpoint is rate-limited — expect fallback to `defaultRates`.
- **Fallback matrix drift.** `src/lib/rates.ts` is hand-maintained, independent of
  the catalog/live math, and holds retired assets (`ZHUJIAO`, `KFC`, `IN11`,
  `ROLEX`) absent from `CURRENCY_CONFIG`. If you add/remove a catalog asset, hand-
  update this file too or the fallback goes stale/incomplete.
- **Cross-rates pivot on USD.** Products are normalized to USD first
  (`getProductPrices`), then cross-rated. Unhandled pairs return `0`; `convert`
  renders a missing/`0` rate as blank/zero, never an error.
- **Distinct currency per row** is enforced via `excludeCurrencies` (from
  `getUsedCurrencies`), disabling in-use options in the selector.
- **Deferred conversion**: handlers dispatch `convert` inside `setTimeout(0)` with
  freshly-built `newFields`; `convert` early-returns while `updating` is true.
- No database, no localStorage — state is ephemeral and resets on reload.
- Lint is `next lint`, but source uses Biome ignore directives — mixed tooling.
  `removeConsole` is commented out, so console logs ship.

## Common commands

```bash
pnpm --filter @cdlab/values dev        # http://values.localhost:3355
pnpm --filter @cdlab/values build      # next build → static export in out/
pnpm --filter @cdlab/values build:cf   # @cloudflare/next-on-pages (Pages bundle)
pnpm --filter @cdlab/values typecheck  # tsc --noEmit
pnpm --filter @cdlab/values lint       # next lint
```

No test script exists.
